name: veribench_three_example_ebm_pilot
purpose: Tiny VeriBench seed set for getting an energy-model experiment running before scaling to a benchmark split.

split_policy.pilot: Use all three examples for debugging the pipeline, overfit checks, and qualitative ranking behavior.
split_policy.real_experiment: Once the pipeline runs, reserve most VeriBench tasks for test/evaluation and use a small train/dev slice only for energy-model fitting and hyperparameter decisions.

examples.why: Minimal arithmetic task; useful for verifying the scorer, candidate formatting, and positive/negative energy ordering.
Nontrivial helper structure with digit/string-style reasoning; catches whether the energy function learns more than arithmetic surface forms.
Classic algorithm with sorted-input precondition, index soundness, and completeness flavor; good first test of whether EBM ranking helps on real specification structure.

first_objective: Rank a small candidate set per task so gold or verifier-passing candidates have lower energy than corrupted or verifier-failing candidates.
negative_sources: syntactic corruptions of theorem statements or proofs
wrong implementation/spec pairings across the three tasks
short-run MCMC or local edits over candidate text once the static ranking setup works
